notes: Thin cell: the smallest round-trip (one plain-text prompt, one assistant reply, no tool use) whose ONLY extra assertion over basic-turn is that the daemon extracted a model_name. opencode runs the turn headlessly via a blocking `opencode run` (no --model flag, so it uses the configured default); session state lives in opencode's SQLite store (~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db), observed by the daemon's ProcessOwnedStore source as a UUID-keyed ses_… session. WHERE THE MODEL CLAIM LIVES: opencode's metrics path reads the active model at message.data.model.modelID (fallback top-level modelID) → tailer.NormalizeModelName → SessionMetrics.ModelName, and ComputeContextUtilization derives ContextWindow from that model (metrics.go:123-131,255-256). That metadata is METRICS-level: the expected.jsonl phase DSL (validate/expected.go Phase struct: phase / expected_state / kind / max_delay_ms / duration_at_least_ms / same_session_as / new_session / invariants) operates over the lifecycle events.jsonl stream, which carries NO model_name field — so the model identification itself is NOT expressible as a phase here. It surfaces at summary.model_name in the replay golden (which replay-fixtures.sh diffs) plus the opencode metrics unit tests; this worktree's opencode default boots on the local LM Studio model whose modelID normalizes to 'gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored', so the metrics-level assertion is that a model_name IS present (non-empty, not 'unknown'), with the concrete string being the configured default model. expected.jsonl therefore asserts only the standard basic-turn lifecycle skeleton: the canonical ready→working→ready arc, never entering waiting. SESSION IDENTITY: session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession (if the process scanner emits one) cannot steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; turn1_start (matching working) establishes the ses_… session identity that turn1_done chains off via same_session_as. The `no state_transition to waiting` invariant covers tool_calls_max:0 — a no-tool turn never gates on a permission ask. The local gemma model is small and slow (~30-45s per turn), so assert model/context only AFTER turn1_done (ContextWindow may briefly read 0 between session detection and the first assistant event). HOW runs differ: the concrete model value follows the configured default, exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, and sub-second timing vary. Stable structurally: one ready→working→ready arc on a single ses_… session, never entering waiting, a non-empty model_name in the golden.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch; never enters waiting (no tool use)
User prompt drives the ses_… session ready → working
The single turn completes back to ready on the same ses_… session; SessionMetrics.ModelName is the configured default model (not 'unknown'), evidenced at summary.model_name in the replay golden
